_**User (2026-03-02 02:17:56Z)**_

Sooooo Apparently you were entirely wrong and this was the fix:

In DebugMotherboard.FetchCPUData you skip the method entirely if GameManager.IsBatchMode is true, so it never reads the chip data on dedicated server

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_





---

_**User (2026-03-02 02:20:18Z)**_

Very basically describe to me why you didn't find that fix and also what in general terms (to a novice programmer) the correct fix was vs the wrong one

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_



## Why I Missed It

I traced the data flow starting from the **client side** — I saw that `ReadProcessorState()` (which converts buffers into display strings) was only called from tick methods that don't run on clients. That was a real observation, but I stopped there and assumed the network data was arriving fine. I never checked whether the **server was actually populating the data in the first place**.

If I had looked one step earlier in the pipeline — at `FetchCPUData()`, which reads the chip's registers and code — I would have seen the `GameManager.IsBatchMode` guard that skips the entire method on dedicated servers. No data in → no data sent → no data displayed. I found a symptom downstream instead of the cause upstream.

## The Wrong Fix vs. The Right Fix (Simple Terms)

Think of it like a restaurant:

- **The kitchen** (`FetchCPUData`) reads ingredients from the fridge (the IC10 chip) and prepares a meal (fills data buffers)
- **The delivery driver** (`BuildUpdate`/`ProcessUpdate`) carries the meal to the customer's house (sends data over the network)
- **The customer** (`ReadProcessorState`) takes the meal out of the delivery bag and puts it on a plate (formats raw data into display text)

**My wrong fix:** I assumed the kitchen was cooking and the delivery was working, but the customer forgot to unpack the bag. So I added extra "unpack the bag" logic on the customer's end.

**The real problem:** The kitchen had a sign on the door saying "CLOSED on dedicated servers" (`if (GameManager.IsBatchMode) return`). The meal was never cooked, the delivery bag was empty, and nothing I did on the customer's end would fix that.

**The correct fix:** Remove the "CLOSED" sign so the kitchen actually cooks the food. Once the data is populated on the server, the existing delivery and display code works fine on its own.

---

_**User (2026-03-03 00:25:40Z)**_

This is from the developer of the mod: 

She is basically saying that this is too performance heavy to work properly. Is there another method? Can you figure out a way to optimize this?

I'm not adding the "UpdateEachFrame". The whole point with running it on the Tick is because it runs on the actual game tick (and after its done) and only 2 times per second.
Each frame runs 60-120 times per second creating massive overhead and probably severly impacts frame rate
And I'm fairly sure the debugcartridge tick runs on clients, otherwise the debug cartridge would never update its screen. Which I'm fairly sure it does

_**User (2026-03-03 05:50:04Z)**_

Will the changes you made break the fix though?

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_
